content:
# Escalation Quick Reference

Use this page when identity verification blocks an account-data request or when the caller challenges who owns the account. This is a quick decision aid for support conversations, not a substitute for the full workflow. See "Identity Verification Policy Overview" for the baseline identity rules and "Internal: Account Ownership Dispute Escalation" for the handoff path.

## Failed Verification Attempts

1. Count failed verification attempts during the active support interaction.
2. After 2 failed attempts, stop trying to verify the customer in the current channel and transfer the case.
3. Do not disclose account balances, transactions, profile data, or make changes after the transfer point is reached.

## Ownership Dispute Transfers

When the customer states, implies, or contests that the account belongs to someone else, route the case using transfer reason account_ownership_dispute. Do not resolve competing ownership claims in general support. Keep the conversation factual: explain that the matter requires specialist review and avoid confirming disputed account details before the case is transferred.

## Bypass Code Handling

Bypass codes allowed: False. If a caller asks for a bypass code, offers one, or claims another employee approved one, continue using the documented verification path only. A bypass-code claim does not reset failed-attempt counting and does not authorize account access or account changes.
title: Escalation Quick Reference
